channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India likely to Revive K30 Biho Air Defence Deal with Koreans
item.description: India’s long-delayed Self-Propelled Air Defence Gun Missile System (SPAD-GMS) program appears to be regaining momentum, with fresh indications that New Delhi and Seoul are exploring a revival of the K30 Biho (Flying Tiger) deal. Sources indicate that the Indian Army could finally move forward on the procurement of 104 systems, a contract originally valued at around $2.6 billion, which has remained stalled despite the K30 Biho being technically selected as the sole qualified bidder in 2018 after outperforming Russia’s Tunguska-M1 and Pantsir systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jammu-start up Machfly Aerospace Unveil ‘SkyReaper’ Jet-Powered UCAV for High-Altitude Deep Strike Missions
item.description: An emerging collaboration involving a college lab at IIT Jammu and startup Machfly Aerospace (SAP) is pushing the boundaries of indigenous unmanned combat systems with the development of SkyReaper, a jet-powered Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicle (UCAV) designed for high-speed, high-altitude, and long-range missions in contested environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why the Su-57 is a 20th-Century Solution to a 21st-Century Problem: The Case for AMCA First
item.description: The prospect of Pakistan inducting a Chinese-origin J-31/J-35 has revived calls in some quarters for India to fast-track a fifth-generation counter by acquiring Russia’s Su-57. On the surface, it appears like a straightforward parity play. In reality, it risks locking the Indian Air Force into a costly, sub-optimal pathway just as India is restructuring its aerospace ecosystem around the AMCA and a private-sector-led production model. The more relevant question is not whether the Su-57 is capable—it is—but whether it aligns with India’s long-term doctrine, industrial strategy, and operational requirements in a battlespace defined by stealth, sensors, and networked warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: How L&T Transformed South Korea’s K-9 Thunder into India’s Battle-Proven K-9 Vajra
item.description: When the South Korean K-9 Thunder was first pitched to India, it was not a straightforward case of importing a ready-made self-propelled howitzer. The program’s eventual success hinged on a deeper, often underappreciated factor—Larsen & Toubro’s (L&T) role as a systems integrator and operational translator, bridging the gap between a foreign design and uniquely Indian battlefield requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Not Logistics Trucks: PAF Video Suggests Nur Khan Targets Were Turkish Command Centres
item.description: In a fresh public relations video released by the Pakistan Air Force, an image has surfaced showing what appear to be sophisticated command and control trailers. These units bear a striking resemblance to the two special-purpose vehicles destroyed by the Indian Air Force in a precision strike on Nur Khan Airbase on 10 May 2025 during Operation Sindoor. The revelation undermines Pakistan’s earlier attempts to downplay the damage as limited to “logistical trailers,” confirming instead that the IAF successfully neutralised high-value Next-Generation Mobile Mission Control Centres (NG-MMCC).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 4D Printing in Defence: How Smart Materials Could Transform India’s Military Platforms and Logistics
item.description: 4D printing is emerging as a breakthrough in advanced manufacturing, introducing a new dimension to how military systems are designed, deployed, and sustained. Unlike traditional 3D printing, which produces static components, 4D printing integrates smart materials that can change shape, strength, or functionality over time when exposed to external stimuli such as heat, pressure, or moisture. This ability to “program” materials at a molecular level effectively turns passive components into adaptive systems, capable of responding dynamically to their operating environment.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Quantum Leap: QNu Labs Targets Military-Grade Networks Beyond 1,000 km Under National Quantum Mission
item.description: India’s push toward quantum-secure communications is entering an operational phase, with Bengaluru-based deeptech firm QNu Labs preparing to scale its quantum key distribution (QKD) networks beyond the recently demonstrated 1,000-km milestone. Riding on the success achieved under the National Quantum Mission, the company is now aligning its next phase of deployments with the requirements of India’s defence forces, where secure, tamper-proof communication is not just desirable but mission-critical.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF launches 2-day air exercise on Purvanchal Expressway airstrip in UP’s Sultanpur
item.description: A two-day Indian Air Force (IAF) exercise featuring fighter-jet operations began on Wednesday on an emergency airstrip along the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradesh’s Sultanpur district, with authorities tightening security and clamping traffic restrictions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Heinous Designs Of Terrorists Will Never Succeed: PM Modi On Pahalgam Anniversary
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday stated that the country will always remember the sacrifices of the innocent civilians who were brutally murdered by terrorists in Pahalgam last year, and he pledged that India will never submit to any form of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Stands Steadfast In Its Resolve To Counter Terrorism: EAM Jaishankar On Pahalgam Attack Anniversary
item.description: External Affairs Minister (EAM) S Jaishankar on Wednesday remembered the victims of the dastardly Pahalgam terror attack, in which 26 innocent people were killed by Pakistan-based terrorists, and reaffirmed India’s resolve to counter terrorism in all its forms and manifestations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pahalgam Horror: India’s Firm Response Forced ISI To Pause More Tourist-Targeted Attacks
item.description: Pakistan needed a decisive response following the dastardly Pahalgam terror attack and it got the one from India, timely and decisively. However, this was not just one location on terrorists’ radar. According to information picked by Intelligence agencies, the ISI had planned more such attacks in Jammu and Kashmir, with an intent to terrorise the tourists and bleed the tourism industry.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Operation Mahadev: Kashmir Forests No Longer A Terrorist Safe Haven
item.description: Dense forest cover in Kashmir has long been used by terrorists to evade security forces after carrying out attacks. The thick foliage and difficult terrain have made counter-operations extremely challenging for the security forces, often turning such missions into prolonged and complex exercises.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: International Solidarity Pours In As India Marks One Year Of Pahalgam Terror Attack
item.description: Marking the first anniversary of the horrific Pahalgam terror attack, diplomatic missions and foreign envoys remembered the victims and expressed solidarity with India in the fight against terrorism. India is observing one year of the deadly Pahalgam terror attack, in which Pakistan-backed terrorists gunned down 26 innocent tourists after identifying their religion, in one of the most brutal incidents targeting civilians in recent years.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DIG reviews security in Samba, calls for heightened alertness, intensified patrolling
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir DIG Shridhar Patil reviewed the security situation in the Samba district and called for strict vigilance, heightened alertness and intensified patrolling to thwart any nefarious designs of anti-national elements. Patil, who oversees the sensitive border districts of Jammu, Samba and Kathua districts, chaired a comprehensive security-cum-crime review meeting with officers of the district police, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Man detained near International Border in Jammu
item.description: A 50-year-old man was detained by the BSF after he was found moving under suspicious circumstances in a forward area near the International Border here, officials said on Tuesday. The man was spotted by a Border Security Force (BSF) patrol in the Mayan Border Out Post area in Makwal forward belt of sub-sector Phallian Mandal on Monday evening and was subsequently taken into custody after failing to provide a satisfactory explanation for his presence in the sensitive zone, the officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
